feed.title: SAP Community - PLM EAM/Plant Maintenance
feed.subtitle: PLM EAM/Plant Maintenance Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Data Definition for Functional Location Master Data
entry.content:
Hi, I am creating a data definition document for Functional Location Master data to identify fields which are required to populate the data as part of migration from ECC to S/4 HANA. I need support on below queries:

1. How to identify total number of tables applicable for functional location

2. How to identify which tables and which fields in those tables feed data to Functional Location T-code IL01

3. Mapping of the tables 

4. Out of those fields which one are mandatory, optional and display

5. Which of these fields need configuration and which do not. 

Is there a way or T-code apart from SE16N which I can leverage for these tasks. Please share any SAP links or best practices on data mapping documen from ECC to S4 HANA for master data elements.

entry.title: Restriction of permissions for notifications with code group "OP-Change" in transactions IW21, IW22
entry.content:
1. Introduction
As part of the internal change management process at Schluchseewerk in the context of S/4HANA, the functionality "Notifications" in the SAP module EAM is utilized. For the operational implementation of this process, we have planned the use of OP notifications. A permission concept has been created for these and partially implemented technically.

During the implementation, an individual role Z_EAM_NOTIFICATION_OP was created, tailored to specific permissions within the notification processing framework.

2. Justification
To ensure a clean and controlled change management process, it is imperative that only authorized users with specific roles have access to OP notifications. In particular, it must be prevented that users without the appropriate authorization can:
- create (transaction IW21),
- View (transaction IW23), or
- change (transaction IW22) OP notifications with the code group (QMGRP) "OP-Change."

Adhering to this restriction is central to quality assurance, traceability of changes, and minimizing potential errors in the change management process.

3. Objective
The goal is to adjust the permissions in SAP so that notifications with the code group OP-Change can only be created, viewed, or changed by authorized individuals. For users with the appropriate permissions for the role, the following restrictions should apply:
- The code group OP-Change should not be visible in the catalog selection,
- it should not be used for creating notifications,
- existing notifications of this group should neither be displayed nor edited unless the user has the appropriate permissions.

4. Technical Issue
In the course of the previous permission check, the authorization object Q_GP_CODE – Use of group codes was already employed. This made it possible to hide the code group OP-Change in the catalog selection and thus prevent the creation of corresponding notifications. However, it remains technically possible to display or change notifications of this code group via IW22 and IW23.

When an OP notification with the coding OP-ART is opened and the user attempts to manually enter the value into the OP-CHANGE field (since the code group OP-Change is already hidden in the catalog selection), the following error message still occurs: When saving in IW22, the system only accesses the authorization object I_QMEL – PM/QM: Notification types, which does not allow restriction at the code group level.

Thus, there is currently a lack of a permission check that ensures at the code group level that notifications with the group "OP-Change" can only be displayed or changed by authorized users.

5. Expectation / Next Steps
We request an examination of how permission control at the code group level for the mentioned transactions can be implemented – either through a suitable combination of existing authorization objects or through alternative technical measures (e.g., user exits, BAdIs, etc.).

The goal is a clean and audit-proof implementation of the requirement to restrict the editing and visibility of "OP-Change" notifications to the defined roles.

entry.title: Pl Deliver time time option in external service PR not working in work order
entry.content:
Pl Deliver time time option in external service PR not working . 

i am trying understand Pl Deliver time how to activate there is any configuration based on control key . 

When we create an external PR from a work order, there's a field called "Planned Delivery Time". Ideally, we should be able to mention the number of days there, and based on that, the delivery date should be updated in the Purchase requi. 

please help for this looking solution 

ex. today date PR creation date 14.5.2025 , if we enter 4 in PL deliv. time then PR delivery date will update as 18.05.2025

entry.title: Alphanumeric number ranges for Equipment in Plant Maintenance
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,
I have huge dump of equipment's, either of them is available or lost as it was created long back ago.

I want to recount them and creates new equipment number, which requires Alpha numeric number ranges.

Ex. AB00000 to AB99999.

Because when I will plan for order or plan, I will sure that this has been created recently and I have to use it.

While I am searching an equipment I found a lot of equipment which is not important to me so I will select recently created with Alpha numeric number series.

Please give me you opinion

entry.title: Shift Factor Behavior When Only Early Completion Is Defined
entry.content:
Let’s say I have a strategy-based maintenance plan with the following settings:

  • Cycle = 10 days

  • Shift factor for early completion = 100%

  • Shift factor for late completion = (left blank)

  • Tolerance = 0% (so any variance will trigger a shift)

📅 The order was completed 5 days late.

Now I’m trying to understand how SAP handles the next planned date.

❓Question:

Since only the shift factor for early completion is maintained, and the order was completed late, will SAP apply the 100% early shift factor to this late completion?

If yes — that would mean:

  • Next plan date is shifted 5 days later (i.e., same effect as if late shift factor = 100%)

If no — then what fallback logic is applied in this case?

📘 What I Know So Far:

  • In IP11, I can define separate shift factors for early and late.

  • But some sources and instructor notes suggest that if only one shift factor is defined, SAP will apply that value for both early and late completions.

  • Others suggest late completions are only shifted if the late shift factor is explicitly defined.

I'm looking for confirmation from anyone who has tested this in a live system or encountered it in certification prep.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: EAM Implementation for Healthcare Facilities and Medical Equipment
entry.content:
Hello Everyone ,

I hope you're doing well.

I’m reaching out to gain a better understanding of how to implement Enterprise Asset Management (EAM) in healthcare facilities, particularly for managing medical equipment. I would appreciate it if you could share the key steps and processes typically followed in such implementations.

Thank you in advance for your valuable guidance.

entry.title: Request for Order Settlement Based on GL Account to Different WBS Elements
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We would like to inquire if it's possible to configure an order settlement rule that allows cost settlement based on General Ledger (GL) accounts to different WBS elements. Our business requirement is as follows:

  • Diesel costs (e.g., posted to a specific GL account) should be settled to WBS Element A (e.g., for fuel-related costs).

  • Spare parts costs (posted to another GL account) should be settled to WBS Element B (e.g., for material/repair costs).

In essence, we need to split the settlement of an order based on the cost origin (GL account) so that each type of cost is settled to the correct corresponding WBS element.

We request your guidance on whether this can be achieved via standard SAP functionality, such as:

  • Creating multiple settlement rules with account assignment filters (GL-based)?

  • Using source structures and assignment rules in settlement profiles?

  • Any enhancement/BAdI recommendations, if standard functionality does not support this?

Your assistance on how best to approach and configure this scenario would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Remove Material Type ERSA in Unplanned Maintenance Components
entry.content:
Hello community,

I have a question regarding the app "Perform Maintenance Job":

When a user adds an unplannend component the material type has the default value "ERSA". We are not using any ERSA materials and therefore no results appear. 

Is there an option to remove or change this default value? Please see also screenshot attached.

 

Thank you and BR
Mona

entry.title: How to Fix Duplicate Component Lines in IW32 , IW31
entry.content:
Issue: In TCode IW32 OR IW31 (Maintenance Order), when adding a component in the Components tab, two identical lines were created in the RESB table—one with a Storage Location (LGORT) and one without. This happened when the Storage Location field was left empty, triggering a Warning message. After filling LGORT, a duplicate line was added.

Root Cause: The issue was traced to a CHAIN in the SAPLCOMK program (Screen 2030, Module MANDATORY_FIELD_CHK). A Warning message (Application Area: EAM_EWM_INTEG_PROC, Message Number: 000) caused the main loop to restart after LGORT was entered, creating a duplicate entry in RESB.

Solution:

  1. Go to TCode SM30.

  2. Enter the view V_160M_PM_1 and select Maintain.

  3. Create the message:

    • Application Area: EAM_EWM_INTEG_PROC

    • Message Number: 000

    • Message Type: I (information)

  4. Save and create a Transport Request.

  5. Test in IW32 to confirm only one line is created in RESB.

Why It Works: Changing the message to Information prevents the program from restarting the loop after LGORT is filled, avoiding duplicate entries.

Tips:

  • Verify the change in a Sandbox environment with different materials and Plants.

  • Check SAP Notes for similar issues (search “SAPLCOMK V_160M_PM_1 EAM_EWM_INTEG_PROC”).

  • Debug SAPLCOMK (Screen 2030) if the issue persists.

Has anyone else faced this in SAP PM? Share your fixes or questions below!

Solution by Ebrahim Heydari

entry.title: SAP Plant Maintenance Configuration Book
entry.content:
Hello, 

Can anyone please share the pdf version of Latest edition copy of SAP Plant Maintenance Configurations Book.

Configuring Plant Maintenance in SAP S/4HANA (Latest Edition) - 2024 by Rheinwerk Publishing Inc., Boston (MA)

Thanks 

Sravan

entry.title: What is the importance of Releasing the maintenance order?
entry.content:
Hello,

I need blow clarification regarding the release of Maintenance order.

I have created maintenance order and Service Purchase requisition (PR) but I have not released maintenance order. the Purchase team member has created Purchase order according to this PR.

My confusion is that It is right business process or wrong one

I think to create PO we have to release the maintenance order first. if Maintenance order is not released then system should pass the error.

Please correct my understanding if wrong.

Thank you..

entry.title: After maintenance plan, auto e-mail triggering is possible for separately for call date and planned
entry.content:
After maintenance plan scheduled, Maintenance notifications/orders, Auto e-mail triggering is possible separately for call date and planned date...?

Thanks in advance...

entry.title: Validation Required: Block Diesel Posting for Equipment in 'Surplus' User Status
entry.content:
We need to implement a validation in the diesel posting process to prevent fuel entries for equipment when the equipment's user status is updated to 'Surplus'.

Requirement:

  • When an equipment has the user status 'Surplus', the system should block any diesel/fuel issue in work order.

  • A relevant error message should be displayed (e.g., "Diesel posting not allowed. Equipment is marked as Surplus.").

entry.title: Operations line deleted after CAT2
entry.content:
Hi Team,

The time entry was done in CAT2 for a particular operation 0010 for a work order. After that someone deleted the operation 0010. Post this we are unable to do CAT9 entry or reverse the time entry in CATS as we are getting message System Status DLT is active. Also we don't have the option to undelete the operations. Please can you guide us on this. We are using S4 Core 108 Version.

Regards,

Jaya

entry.title: No deja asingar la persona responsable en la app asignación de operaciones de ordenes de mtto
entry.content:
Hola

La app F3423 no esta dejando relacionar la persona responsable a las operaciones de las ordenes filtradas según el periodo de búsqueda.

entry.title: It doesn't allow to assign the responsible person in the app for assigning maintenance order operat.
entry.content:
Hello

The F3423 app is not allowing the responsible person to be linked to the operations of the filtered orders based on the search period. I understand that the app doesn't search for the person linked to the job position; instead, it must be created using the F3888 "Team and Responsibilities Management" app. Could you please tell me how to create a team using the latter app?

entry.title: IP30 doesn't reschedule maintenance plan as there is an order TECOed
entry.content:
Hi All:

We use IP30 mass schedule maintenance plan. 

We have maintenance order converted from call and TECOed, in order to let new planning follow up with planning key date but not TECO date, we change 2 "Shift Factor" in maintenance plan.

We tested the changes using IP30 in EAQ, maintenance plan is correctly planned at planning key date but not TECO date.

But as we made the change in PRD after a few of days, we found IP30 doesn't reschedule maintenance plan at planning key day but still on TECO date, however, IP10 can reschedule maintenance plan at planning key day but not on TECO date any more. And further at this time we go back to EAQ to test, we found IP10 can  reschedule maintenance plan at planning key day but not on TECO date any more, IP30 doesn't work longer.

Appreciate any sharing.

Yinyun

entry.title: BTP Workzone (standard) update config to allow additional origin
entry.content:
Hi,

Anyone has idea how to configure BTP/BTP Workzone to allow additional origin called from the third-party URL?

Scenario below: Using OCI catalog punch out scenario -> that Workzone hosted the S/4 Standard App- Webdynpro Change maintenance order App will launch a third-party catalog in a new tab when user click on select catalog button in the component screen, and the third-party catalog URL will post the selected data back to SAP via hook URL maintained in the configuration in table WSI_CALLSTRUC.

The hook URL we have maintained the BTP Workzone backend destination URL: for example:
https://<subaccount-host>-sapdelim-s4d05rt500.launchpad.cfapps.ca10.hana.ondemand.com/sap/bc/rest/sap/oci_catalog

where this URL is where we observe how the workzone route other odata Call from workzone to backend S/4.

We are getting the error below from the BTP response after user click submit the cart in the catalog page that trigger to the HOOK_URL.

Note: We found out this error is responded by the BTP, not by the local browser, hence something needs to be done in BTP to allow this origin, not the normal CORS blocked by the common browser scenario.

What we found out further due to the third party will set the HTTP request header "Origin":"https://www.grainger.ca" in the hook URL request that is causing the issue where blocked by BTP/BTP workzone.

We have try simulate the same request from postman, able to get the same error. However, once we remove the Orgin from the HTTP header, it is able to post to the backend with 200 error success.

So far found this note 3169523 - Configure Tenant Security Header for CAI - SAP for Me that is allow us to configure additional origin, but is applicable for CAI only, not for Workzone.

Thanks.

entry.title: Impact of Changing Cost Center on Settlement Rule in IW32 After Order Release
entry.content: In transaction IW32, once the order has been released and a settlement rule has already been assigned, my question is: if I change the cost center in the “Location” – “Account Assignment” view, should this change be reflected in the settlement rule?

entry.title: Maintenance notifications app short dump
entry.content:

A new error came up when we tried to use the Find Maintenance Notifications app (F2071).
The error is:

I have already tried to search up for an sap note but I was not succeded.
Can any of you help me?

Thank you! 🙂
